words: This side of the following tape was recorded on Duly 5, 1973 at the home of
fir. Rudolph Nordby in Gene see, Idaho. The voices on the tape arg those of
Rudolph Nordby and the interviewer, Rob Moore.

speaker: Interviewer
words: The story you told about being dropped off at bhe farm place in

words: Palouse was pretty interesting. I wonder if you could talk of

speaker: Interviewer
words: that again.

speaker: Interviewer
words: Mr. Nordby: When we moved in there?

speaker: Interviewer
words: Right, when you came by train into Palouse and what happened then.

speaker: Interviewer
words: MR. NORDBY:   We carne in by train into Palouse and then of course, hired a cab

speaker: Interviewer
words: with a driver to take us out. This was in February and the roads

speaker: Interviewer
words: were just breaking up. Great big holes and we'd be going along

speaker: Interviewer
words: there and those holes would be full of water and he'd be driving

speaker: Interviewer
words: and he'd go bingoi down in those holes.(chuckles) And of course,

speaker: Interviewer
words: the interesting thing about that foas that my grandmother was

speaker: Interviewer
words: there. Of course, she talked nothing but Norwegian. And she scolded

speaker: Interviewer
words: this fellow that was driving so much, about his carelessness in

speaker: Interviewer
words: driving, see? So it went on for quite awhile and pretty soon he...

speaker: Interviewer
words: his name was Oohnson.

words: INTERVIEWER:   Did he speak Norwegian?

speaker: Interviewer
words: MR. NORDBY:   He was a Norwegian.(laughs)she of course had no idea that the

words: Norwegians had ever gotten out here. But it was kind of interesting.

words: It was a six and a half mile drive.

speaker: Interviewer
words: he spoke Norwegian?

words: she felt so terrible.

words: That'd be eight. It made quite a hackload.

words: day. That's about a day's drive right there.
